reasoning: The stock recently experienced a rapid spike, closing on Feb 28 at $70.30, up from ~$67.50 slightly over a week prior. Due to anticipated mean reversion after this un-catalized move, and following the yfinance tool's statistical projection, short-term probabilities stay under 50% (0.45 for March 9). Additionally, Equity Residential holds its Q1 ex-dividend date in late March (historically March 22-24), which will artificially drop the price by ~$0.675 (~1%) right before the April 1 target date, making it less likely to be above the local peak (0.43). For the longer-horizon dates, underlying long-term market drift (capital appreciation of ~4-6% annualized after netting out discrete dividend payouts) steadily offsets early headwinds. Hence, the probabilities cross 0.50 smoothly towards the 1-year mark.
